original_prompt: Perform the following tasks in the order they are given: 1. How many appointments do I have on May 15th, 2024? Please give me a summary of the description for each appointment. 2. Please get me the link to reset my TechServices password. It should be in an email I received recently.
assessment_reasoning: The prompt is clear about the two distinct tasks and their order: count appointments on May 15, 2024 and summarize each appointment description; then find a TechServices password-reset link in a recent email. Minor ambiguities remain: which calendar/mailbox to search (if multiple), timezone for May 15th, what timeframe “recently” means, how detailed the “summary” should be, and whether variations in the service name (e.g., “Tech Services” vs “TechServices”) matter. These are small execution details but do not prevent performing the tasks.

variations.L1: Check my calendar and tell me what you find for May 15, 2024. Also look in my recent email for a TechServices password reset link and give it to me.
variations.L2: Please do these in order: 1) How many appointments do I have on May 15th, 2024? Give a short summary of the description for each appointment. 2) Find the link to reset my TechServices password — it should be in an email I received recently. Tell me the link.
variations.L3: Perform the following in order: 1) Count all appointments on May 15, 2024 in my primary calendar and give a one-sentence summary of the description for each appointment. 2) Search my inbox (last 14 days) for an email that contains a TechServices password reset link and return the link plus the email subject and date. If you can't find it, say so.
variations.L4: Perform the following tasks in the order they are given: 1. How many appointments do I have on May 15th, 2024? Please give me a summary of the description for each appointment. 2. Please get me the link to reset my TechServices password. It should be in an email I received recently.
variations.L5: Do these two tasks step-by-step and return the results as structured output: Step 1 — Calendar: Query my primary calendar for events on 2024-05-15 (local time). Return an object with: total_count (integer) and events (array). For each event include: title, start_time (ISO 8601), end_time (ISO 8601), and description_summary (one sentence, ≤30 words) extracted from the event description. Step 2 — Email: Search my inbox folder for messages received in the last 30 days matching subject or body containing 'TechServices' and 'reset' (case-insensitive). From the most recent matching message that contains a URL with 'reset' or 'password' in the path, extract that URL and return an object with: reset_link (exact URL), email_subject, sender_address, received_date (ISO 8601), and a short excerpt (<=200 characters) showing the URL in context. If no matching email or URL is found, return a clear 'not found' result for Step 2. If you lack permission to access calendar or email, state which permission is missing and stop.
